name: Bialys
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups All-purpose flour, or as needed
1 tablespoon Veg. oil (or lard if prefer)
1 1/2 tablespoons Poppy seeds, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1/2 cup of the warm water, the yeast and sugar. Let work until foamy.
2. Mix remaining 1 1/2 cups warm water, salt, gluten flour, and all-purpose flour into the yeast mixture. Knead vigorously on a floured surface until smooth (dough will be soft).
3. Form into a ball and place in a greased bowl with the greased side up. Let rise, covered with plastic, for about 90 minutes, or until tripled in bulk.
4. Punch down the dough, turn over, and let it rise again until doubled.
5. Punch down and roll into 2 cylinders. Cut each into 8 rounds, lay them flat, cover with a towel, and let rest. Meanwhile, prepare the topping by mixing oil, poppy seeds, onion, and salt; set aside.
6. Pat dough into flattened rounds, about 3 1/2 inches in diameter, place on lightly floured board, cover with dry towel, then damp one side and let rise until half-proofed (about 30 minutes).
7. Press the bottom of a shot glass or small jar about 1 inch in diameter in the center of each bialy to make deep indentation.
8. Spread topping over the bialys. Let proof for an additional 15 minutes, then turn front to back for even baking.
9. Carefully transfer each bialy onto large ungreased baking sheets. Bake on the upper and lower shelves of the oven for 6 to 7 minutes, then switch pans and turn front to back for even baking. Bake about 5 to 6 more minutes.
10. Cool on racks. Store in plastic bags in the refrigerator up to 2 days. Freeze for longer storage.
